bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same utility structure, so valueF gives 1.0 utility on every issue. My top bid gives utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. Starting at the best possible position is standard early-game strategy with no time pressure yet.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), so there's no need to concede much. I'll stay close to my top bid but make a small concession on issueA (my least important issue, weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate, while holding firm on issueD (most important, weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3).

The opponent is firmly holding at their maximum utility bid (all valueA) and hasn't conceded at all. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll concede slightly on issueA (my least important issue at 0.1 weight) by dropping to valueD, and keep my high-value choices on issueD (most important) and issueC. This keeps my utility high while signaling willingness to negotiate.

The opponent has been consistently offering valueA for all issues, which gives me very low utility (0.2 across the board = 0.2 total). However, in their latest offer they moved issueD from valueA to valueB, which is a small concession. We're very early (t=0.06), so I should stay firm near my top utility. I'll concede slightly on issueA (my lowest weight issue) while holding firm on issueD and issueC (my highest weight issues). I'll offer issueA: valueC to show some movement while keeping the high-weight issues at valueF.

The opponent is consistently offering valueA for most issues, with a slight concession on issueD (moved from valueA to valueB). They seem to be holding very firm on their preferred values. My utility for their latest offer is: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.4*0.35 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.14 = 0.26, which is quite low. We're still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I've been conceding on issueA (least important to me). I'll now also slightly concede on issueB (second least important) while keeping issueC and issueD at valueF.

The opponent is making very slow concessions - they've only moved issueD from valueA to valueB once, and issueC from valueA to valueB in their latest offer. Their offers give me very low utility (around 0.2). I'm still early in the negotiation (t=0.1), so I don't need to concede heavily yet. My highest weight issue is issueD (0.4), so I should hold firm there. I'll concede slightly on issueA (low weight 0.1) and make a small concession on issueB, while keeping issueC and issueD at high values. I'll target around 0.85+ utility.

The opponent is being very stubborn, mostly offering valueA across all issues with only minor variations (issueD went to valueB once, issueC went to valueB). Their offers give me very low utility (~0.2 across the board). We're still very early (t=0.12), so I don't need to concede much yet. I should hold firm on my most important issues (issueD and issueC) while making small concessions on less important ones (issueA, issueB). My current utility target should remain high (~0.85+). I'll concede slightly on issueB to show movement while keeping issueC and issueD at valueF.

Their offers give me very low utility (~0.2 across the board).
